_comment: FN-1233 — Incident image storage access policy (Roadside v2 Story 3.1 / FN-1195). S3-compatible IAM policy document scoping the upload identity to the tenant-prefixed object space `tenants/<tenantId>/incidents/<incidentId>/...`. Replace <BUCKET> with the per-environment bucket (fleetneuron-incident-images-{dev,staging,prod}). This is the canonical statement of intent and is directly applicable to AWS S3; on Cloudflare R2 (the current backend, see backend/packages/goodmen-shared/storage/r2-storage.js) the same scoping is enforced by (1) a bucket-scoped R2 API token and (2) application-level prefix enforcement in incident-images.service.js (buildS3Key + assertCallAccess). See docs/runbooks/incident-image-storage.md.
